### Notes

- Source checkmarks are historical claims pending evidence and Wave-C audit.
- Stable initiative IDs are defined in the master PRD. Imported source rows use `SRC::<relative-path>::L<line>` identifiers and retain source state.
- Update a source task list immediately after meaningful work, then mirror the state here. Add every discovery at the end as `NEW`.
- Potentially large Supabase reads require verified pagination until a short page; large `.limit()` values are not accepted as completeness evidence.
- The initialization working tree contains extensive pre-existing changes. Inspect the relevant diff before editing and never overwrite unrelated work.
- Parent tasks close only after their subtasks, required verification, cleanup, and synchronization are complete.

## Imported Source Checkbox Ledger

Imported source ledger after pair repair and live discovery: **4,539 rows** from **74 files** (**3,917 checked claims; 622 unchecked**). The original initialization baseline was 3,907 rows from 65 files; repaired-pair rows and live `NEW` findings are appended with stable source ordinals, including A-FORGE-DASH production/rollback work, A-FORGE-CC browser/reconciliation/promotion remediation, the cross-route cron-auth audit and P0 transcript-exposed bearer containment, the sustainability identity gap, A-PRED historical opponent/standings/playoff-scoring/lifecycle corrections, A-XG-TREND registry-coverage/outcome-leakage/flurry/rebound findings, A-DRAFT Yahoo-map/player-master/diagnostic/SSR/persistence/UX findings, A-DRAFT-DEBUG SHA/performance reconciliation, A-AUTH live Vault/configuration/migration/ownership/Fantrax/ESPN/Patreon/callback-fragment/expired-session/autofill-credential reconciliation, the remaining Patreon active-supporter evidence gap, the Lines/CCC X API timestamp build blocker, and newly observed recurring cron database failures, false-success paths, privileged-RPC exposure, and stale-prune access-path gaps through 2026-07-14. `C####` is the stable within-source ordinal captured at import; the original line number is evidence only and may drift as source lists are maintained. The six rows from `tasks/TASKS/rules/generate-tasks.mdc` are reference-template examples, classified as non-actionable reference rows rather than implementation work.

- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0148` (original L290) — 28.4 Include last-updated metadata for ranking snapshots and metric methodology. Ranking responses now include `meta.snapshotDate`, the UI displays the selected snapshot date, and metric metadata includes `methodologyUpdatedAt`.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0149` (original L292) — 29.0 NEW: Formalize goalie deployment and quality-start methodology
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0150` (original L293) — 29.1 Verify whether `/trueGoalieValue` already calculates GSAx, quality starts, really bad starts, steals, starts share, or relative goalie metrics in reusable form. Verified `/trueGoalieValue` ranks goalies from weekly aggregates and WGO game rows but does not expose reusable GSAx/QS/starts-share logic; WGO goalie upserts already store classic `quality_start`/`quality_starts_pct`, and generated/live types already expose `nhl_xg_goalie_game_aggregates.goals_saved_above_expected`.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0151` (original L294) — 29.2 Implement adjusted core netshare so emergency-callup starts during contiguous top-two goalie absence windows can be excluded when confidence is sufficient. Added `calculateAdjustedCoreNetshare`, which excludes non-core emergency starts only when the configured top-two goalies are both unavailable with sufficient absence confidence.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0152` (original L295) — 29.3 Support both quality-start compatibility mode and modern xG mode where `GSAx >= 0` defines a quality start. Added `isGoalieQualityStart` with `classic_save_pct` and `modern_gsax` modes, plus `calculateGoalieGsax`, really-bad-start, steal-game, and starts-share bucket helpers.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0153` (original L296) — 29.4 Add tests for injury/call-up denominator logic, starts-share buckets, quality-start modes, and steal-game logic. Added focused goalie methodology tests covering GSAx derivation, modern/classic QS modes, really bad starts, steal games, starts-share buckets, emergency-callup denominator exclusion, and low-confidence denominator retention.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0154` (original L298) — 30.0 NEW: Formalize team-style adjusted metrics and luck formulas
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0155` (original L299) — 30.1 Verify whether score- and venue-adjusted 5v5 team metrics already exist before creating team-style aggregates. Live schema probe found existing `nhl_xg_team_game_aggregates` and `nhl_xg_team_rolling_aggregates` with raw `xg_for`, `xg_against`, `goals_for`, `goals_against`, and `shot_attempts_*`; no score-adjusted or venue-adjusted team xG columns were verified, so no new aggregate table was created.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0156` (original L300) — 30.2 Model run-and-gun as two axes: event rate (`xGF + xGA`) and control (`xGF%`), instead of a single blended label only. Added `calculateRunAndGunProfile`, which returns separate event-rate pace and xGF% control axes.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0157` (original L301) — 30.3 Replace any team-luck formula that rewards `GA/xGA` in the wrong direction with net goals above expected or separate finishing/save luck components. Added `calculateTeamLuckComponents`, with finishing luck as `GF - xGF`, save luck as `xGA - GA`, and net goals above expected as their sum.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0158` (original L302) — 30.4 Add tests for team shot-quality denominator, run-and-gun classification, and team-luck directionality. Added `teamStyleMethodology` tests covering Fenwick shot-quality denominator, separate run-and-gun axes, and positive/negative save-luck directionality.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0159` (original L304) — 31.0 NEW: Version published ranking snapshots and derived methodology

- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0197` (original L351) — 40.3 Implement adjusted defensive impact only after avoiding redundancy with existing projection/game-prediction models and proving the source data supports held-out validation. Verified the repo already had `web/lib/xg/adjustedImpact.ts`, `/api/v1/db/update-nhl-xg-adjusted-impact`, and `migrations/20260527_create_nhl_xg_adjusted_impact_tables.sql`, so no redundant rankings-only table was added. Added chronological held-out validation to the existing adjusted-impact model path, included the validation report in model-run/player provenance, and required it before non-dry-run persistence. Live Supabase verification found approved shot-goal xG predictions (`118,610` rows through `2026-05-26`), shot features (`162,000` rows), and shift rows (`1,052,578` rows through `2026-06-06`). A limited live dry run against `logistic_l2-s20252026-p1-st1-f1-cfg9bac2706` built `2,996` design rows, passed leakage validation, passed chronological holdout validation, and improved validation MSE over the training-mean baseline by `4.076114%`. The live output tables are not installed yet, so publication is tracked in task `83.0`.

- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0335` (original L506) — 57.28 Investigate why the broad bounded 5v5 recompute request for `2026-03-01` through `2026-04-16` did not return promptly from local `curl` after the Gaudreau source fix. The request eventually completed successfully after `19m 50s`, processed `806` players, upserted `12,960` rows, and returned `freshnessBlockers: 0`, so the apparent hang was an unbounded multi-week `daily_incremental` run exceeding its `4m 30s` runtime budget. Added a preflight blocker so explicit multi-week date-window recomputes require `playerId`, `resumeFrom`, `maxPlayers`, `executionProfile=overnight`, or `confirmBroadRun=true`; the problematic URL now returns HTTP `422` immediately with bounding guidance, while explicit overnight full recomputes remain allowed.

Enabled the Teams segment on `/rankings`, hid player-only filters for team mode, added a team matrix table with style badges, PP/PK/trend/context chips, pagination, metric sorting, source warnings, and an explicit raw-vs-adjusted team-style notice; browser verification on `localhost:3002/rankings?entity=teams...` rendered live team rows with no console errors.

- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0347` (original L520) — 59.2 Run approved full rolling recompute after source refresh and PP-goals fallback changes. After adding the PP-combination HTML shift-report fallback and repairing `2026-04-12` through `2026-04-16`, the bounded latest-source rolling recompute upserted `2664` rows for `668` players in `1m 23s` with `coverageWarnings=0`, `freshnessBlockers=0`, freshness gate `PASS`, and HTTP `200`; the prior Postgres row-size error remains mitigated by rolling storage compaction and tracked for durable cleanup in task `73.0`.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0348` (original L521) — 59.3 Verify unpinned latest default requests for ixG/60, shot attempts/60, PP goals/60, and xG finishing metrics no longer require latest-calculable fallback. Local API verification on `2026-06-09` returned `snapshotDate=2026-04-16`, `latestAvailableSnapshotDate=2026-04-16`, and `snapshotSelectionReason=latest_available` for all-situations `ixg_per_60`, all-situations `shot_attempts_per_60`, PP `goals_per_60`, PP `ixg_per_60`, plus xG finishing metrics `expected_shooting_percentage`, `goals_above_expected`, and `unrealized_xg`.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0349` (original L522) — 59.4 Keep fallback-to-latest-calculable behavior as a safety net, but mark freshness fallback clearly in API/UI metadata. The contextual API continues to return `snapshotSelectionReason=latest_calculable_metric` plus a freshness message when fallback is used, and `/rankings` surfaces the API `meta.message` in matrix and Metric Explorer unavailable/fallback states.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0350` (original L524) — 60.0 NEW: Make Results Luck Index live only after frozen-baseline persistence is production-safe
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0351` (original L525) — 60.1 Define the production storage or snapshot source for frozen non-overlapping baselines. The production output remains `skater_composite_ratings.results_luck_index`, but code now locks a persistence contract requiring selected-window exclusion provenance before any non-null value can be written. Live verification on `2026-06-09` found `85,620` `player_baselines` rows with latest snapshot `2026-06-09`, but `0` non-null `skater_composite_ratings.results_luck_index` rows; `player_baselines` is explicitly blocked as a drop-in source because its sustainability payloads do not by themselves prove the selected rankings window was excluded.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0352` (original L526) — 60.2 Wire results_luck_index into the ranking API/matrix only after baseline leakage tests pass against live-like fixtures. Baseline leakage and component-aware scorer fixtures pass, the matrix registry now marks Results Luck as sparse live, and `playerMatrix.ts` now reads/sorts `skater_composite_ratings.results_luck_index` directly instead of falling back to MCM. Live publication on `2026-06-09` upserted `season` and `last20` 5v5 composite rows for all/L1-L4/P1-P3 contexts; SQL verification showed `last20` non-null Results Luck counts of `61` all, `13` L1, `15` L2, `9` L3, `5` L4, `12` P1, `3` P2, and `4` P3. Season rows intentionally remain null because the selected-window-excluded baseline gate is not valid for a full-season current window.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0353` (original L527) — 60.3 Add UI copy explaining “hot/lucky” versus “sustainable” and selected-window exclusion. The planned Results Luck matrix column now explains that values above `100` mean current results are running hotter than a selected-window-excluded baseline, and its planned reason explicitly blocks publication until frozen baseline provenance proves the selected window is excluded.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0354` (original L528) — 60.4 Add tests proving current-window values do not leak into the baseline. Existing focused coverage in `resultsLuckIndex.test.ts` verifies the selected current window is subtracted from the season numerator/denominator before calculating the baseline, covers thin-baseline peer blending, and covers peer fallback when a matching season baseline is unavailable; the test was rerun with the updated persistence contract.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0355` (original L530) — 61.0 NEW: Decide and document adjusted team-style and adjusted defensive-model roadmap
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0356` (original L531) — 61.1 For team style, either implement score/venue-adjusted 5v5 aggregates or label current team style as raw/contextual. Added `ADJUSTED_DEFENSE_MODEL_ROADMAP` documenting current source status: NHL on-ice attribution utilities exist, NST 5v5 zone-start context exists, score-state helpers exist, and `rolling_player_game_metrics` supports strength states, but no persisted adjusted defensive rankings design matrix/source is wired yet.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0358` (original L533) — 61.3 Add validation criteria before any adjusted model is allowed to replace contextual labels. The roadmap now requires chronological held-out validation, improvement over raw on-ice xGA/xGF baselines, regularization/shrinkage, stability checks across TOI/deployment buckets, and provenance proving teammate/opponent/score/zone/rest controls before contextual labels can be replaced.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0359` (original L534) — 61.4 Document methodology limitations in the rankings glossary. Added a `Contextual rankings pipeline` section to `web/README.md` covering source ingestion (`nst_gamelog_*`, `nst_gamelog_5v5_*`, WGO, line/special-teams context), `update-rolling-player-averages` writes to `rolling_player_game_metrics`, runtime ranking APIs read latest per-player rolling snapshots, and composite publishing writes `skater_composite_ratings`.

- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0373` (original L551) — 64.1 Replace repeated per-metric ranking surface builds with a shared in-request ranked snapshot or multi-metric ranking builder. Added `buildContextualRankingsSurfaces` so the matrix fetches the latest-player snapshot once, ranks all requested live metric columns from shared candidates, and keeps each cell's rank/percentile peer-group-relative. Updated matrix tests to guard that the builder is called once for the shared metric set.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0374` (original L552) — 64.2 Consider a persisted latest-player-snapshot table or RPC/view that returns one current rolling row per player per season/strength/as-of date without scanning multiple game dates through PostgREST. Live Supabase verification found no existing latest-player rolling RPC/view. `distinct on (player_id)` and array-date RPC candidates were slower than the app fallback (`~48s` timeout-prone direct plan and `~8-15s` array/lateral plans), so this increment keeps the exact-date fallback and adds a targeted latest-snapshot index migration instead of adopting a slower RPC.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0375` (original L553) — 64.3 Add performance acceptance coverage for the default matrix endpoint so the full-player-pool fix remains under an agreed response-time budget. Added `web/scripts/check-rankings-matrix-performance.ts` and `npm run check:rankings-matrix-performance`; the smoke enforces `<=8s` cold and `<=2s` warm local budgets plus semantic guards for `10` visible rows, `>=700` ranked rows, `>=70` pages, and `>=700` qualified peers.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0376` (original L555) — 65.0 NEW: Investigate cold matrix snapshot fetch performance after the shared multi-metric builder
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0377` (original L556) — 65.1 Evaluate whether a database RPC/view can return the latest row per player using `distinct on (player_id)` or an indexed window query faster than multiple PostgREST date pages. Live `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` showed the direct DB distinct plan scanning much more than expected and timing out around `120s`; a lateral exact-date array plan completed but still took about `8.5s` in-database before returning full rows.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0378` (original L557) — 65.2 Investigate why a batched PostgREST `.in("game_date", dates)` snapshot query returned API failures around `8-9s` locally, while bounded parallel exact-date queries succeeded. The batched form caused API failures locally and live SQL chose broad hash/index scans; exact-date probes continue to use safer indexed reads, so the app keeps bounded parallel exact-date fetching.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0379` (original L558) — 65.3 Set an explicit cold and warm response-time budget for `/api/v1/contextual-rankings/matrix` before closing task `64.3`. Provisional local budget is cold `<=8s` after code reload/cache miss and warm `<=2s` with the short-lived snapshot cache, while preserving `776` ranked 5v5 skaters and peer-group-relative metric counts. `64.3` remains open until this is covered by an automated or repeatable perf smoke.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0380` (original L559) — 65.4 Apply and verify `migrations/20260608_add_rolling_player_latest_snapshot_index.sql`, then re-measure cold `/api/v1/contextual-rankings/matrix` performance and confirm the plan uses `idx_rpgm_latest_snapshot_lookup`. Applied live through Supabase on `2026-06-08`; narrow `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` uses `idx_rpgm_latest_snapshot_lookup`, and `npm run check:rankings-matrix-performance` returned `10` rows, `776` total ranked rows, `78` pages, `776` metric peers, with timings of `7006ms` cold and `1009-1016ms` warm.

- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0414` (original L601) — 73.2 Decide whether to split recompute-only/historical rolling components into a support table, JSONB payload, or narrower compatibility view so populated xG/ICF/PP fields cannot exceed Postgres row-size limits. Decision: keep the primary `rolling_player_game_metrics` row as the narrow latest/ranking-read contract, move recompute-only historical/all/last3/3YA/career/avg compatibility payloads into a keyed support table or JSONB support column/table, and provide a compatibility reader/view only for legacy validation/audit/projection paths that still need the old wide surface. This matches the current temporary compaction behavior, which preserves ranking fields plus PP support fields while nulling storage-heavy historical families.

Live verification on `2026-06-09` found the migration absent from Supabase history and both tables missing, so the existing table migration was applied along with `migrations/20260609214122_enable_rls_nhl_xg_adjusted_impact_tables.sql`. Direct SQL verification confirmed both tables exist, RLS is enabled, `public_read` SELECT policies are present for `anon`/`authenticated`, expected primary/ranking indexes exist, and generated Supabase types now include the live tables.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0459` (original L656) — 83.2 Run a full-season adjusted-impact dry run with the approved shot-goal xG source model, require leakage validation and chronological held-out validation to pass, and record model-run validation metrics before writing rows. Dry run against approved source model `logistic_l2-s20252026-p1-st1-f1-cfg9bac2706`, feature version `1`, season `20252026`, `minPlayerRows=100`, `minValidationRows=5000`, and `iterations=100` returned `118,610` prediction rows, `118,610` feature rows, `1,048,367` shift rows, `117,846` design rows, and `764` skipped design rows. Leakage validation passed with no warnings. Chronological held-out validation passed with `108,188` training rows through `2026-04-11`, `9,658` validation rows from `2026-04-12` through `2026-05-26`, validation MSE `0.023591`, baseline MSE `0.025369`, and MSE improvement `7.008554%`.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0460` (original L657) — 83.3 Populate `nhl_xg_adjusted_impact_model_runs` and `nhl_xg_adjusted_player_impacts` only after task `83.2` passes, then verify row counts, latest provenance, feature version, target family, and player coefficient coverage. The non-dry-run endpoint passed the same leakage/held-out gates and upserted `1` model run plus `802` player-impact rows. Direct SQL verification found target family `on_ice_xg_differential_v1`, source model `logistic_l2-s20252026-p1-st1-f1-cfg9bac2706`, feature version `1`, season `20252026`, `117,846` training rows, `940` training players, model MSE `0.024134`, and `802` player rows with held-out validation provenance set to passed.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0461` (original L658) — 83.4 Decide whether adjusted-impact coefficients should remain a methodology-only diagnostic, become a rankings metadata source, or replace contextual defensive labels for a gated defensive metric; do not surface it on `/rankings` until provenance and validation metadata are available for every published row. Decision for this iteration: keep the adjusted-impact coefficients as a live methodology/diagnostic source only. They are validated and persisted, but they should not replace contextual defensive labels yet because the current model is an on-ice xG differential baseline, not a defense-specific promoted rankings metric with zone-start/rest/stability display contracts.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0462` (original L659) — 83.5 If the adjusted-impact layer is promoted to rankings, add metric registry/API/UI tests proving it is labeled as adjusted, distinct from raw contextual defense, and unavailable when live model provenance is missing or fails validation. Not promoted in this iteration; no `/rankings` registry/API/UI surface was added. Future promotion testing is tracked in task `84.0`.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0463` (original L661) — 84.0 NEW: Promote adjusted-impact coefficients into rankings only after a defense-specific product contract is defined
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0464` (original L662) — 84.1 Review the live `nhl_xg_adjusted_player_impacts` coefficient scale, stability by TOI/deployment bucket, player samples, and sign/direction semantics before deciding any user-facing score transformation. Live review of `802` player-impact rows found coefficient range `-0.001158` to `0.000851`, median `0.000004`, p05 `-0.000526`, p95 `0.00044985`, median sample `1,479` rows, and average approximate standard error `0.00508858`. Joined samples show positive and negative coefficients are not a direct replacement for current contextual Defense Rating: top positive examples include Beck Malenstyn, Travis Sanheim, and Mike Matheson, while top negative examples include Nick Suzuki, Kirill Kaprizov, Cole Caufield, Quinn Hughes, and Lane Hutson. Position bucket review found forwards average `-0.00003202`, defensemen average `0.00003985`, and `77` adjusted-impact rows missing current composite deployment joins.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0465` (original L663) — 84.2 Decide whether the promoted metric is overall adjusted xG impact, defense-only adjusted impact, or a separate methodology annotation for the existing Defense Rating. Decision: do not promote to a user-facing rankings metric in this iteration. The live coefficient is an overall on-ice xG differential baseline with teammate/opponent/context controls, not a defense-only decomposition or a validated replacement for contextual Defense Rating. Treat it as methodology/diagnostic evidence until task `84.3` defines the missing promotion controls.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0466` (original L664) — 84.3 Add missing context controls if required for promotion, especially explicit zone-start/rest controls and a defense-specific target or decomposition if the metric is meant to replace contextual defense. Added `adjustedImpactPromotionContract` and exposed it through rankings metadata so adjusted-impact remains `diagnostic_live` and `blocked_missing_controls` for rankings promotion. Verification found `nhl_xg_shot_features` live payloads include score, strength, shot, shift-age, deployment, and zone-code context, but `0` of `162,000` feature rows contain `ownerRestDays` or `opponentRestDays`; the repo has a travel/fatigue table migration and builder, but the table is not installed live or joined into adjusted-impact rows. NST 5v5 on-ice zone-start tables exist as source candidates but are not transformed into adjusted-impact controls. A defense-specific target/decomposition is still missing, so adjusted-impact must not replace contextual Defense Rating.
- [x] `SRC::tasks/TASKS/contextual-hockey-rankings/tasks-prd-contextual-hockey-rankings.md::C0467` (original L665) — 84.4 Add rankings registry/API/UI tests proving promoted adjusted-impact metrics are labeled as adjusted, distinct from raw contextual defense, and unavailable when live model provenance is missing or fails validation. Because adjusted-impact was not promoted in this iteration, added registry/metadata/API/UI guards instead: metadata exposes `adjustedImpactPromotion.readiness.status=blocked_missing_controls`, the published metric list excludes `adjusted_xg_impact` and `adjusted_defensive_impact`, and the Rankings Metric Explorer test asserts adjusted-impact labels are not rendered from the current metric payload.
